english: °tsal I wish to be treated fairly like any other people Dzl. IV; kă-lo snyóms-pa (ka lo snyoms pa) to regulate (a matter), to manage or direct (a business) justly, uniformly Glr. — snyóm-du med (snyom du med), he has not his like Dzl.; *tag nyóm-la* (*tag nyom la*) C. always uniformly, without variation. II. sbst., also btaṅ-snyóms (btang snyoms), evenness, or calmness of mind, equanimity, snyóms-par jùg-pa (snyoms par jug pa) to assume it, = sems mnyám-par bźág-pa (sems mnyam par bzhag pa), v. mnyám-pa (mnyam pa). — snyoms-jùg byèd-pa (snyoms jug byed pa) 1. id., 2. euphem. for krig-pa spyód-pa (khrig pa spyod pa). — mgo-snyóms (mgo snyoms) impartial Mil. — snyóms-po (snyoms po) equal, even, uniform, e.g. in every part equally thick.

pf. and fut. bsnyal (bsnyal) (cf. nyál-ba (nyal ba)) 1. to lay down; to bed a person, to assign him his couch or bed Pth.; *ṭú-gu mál-du* (*tu gu mal du*) C. (to lay) a child on its bed, to put to bed; *nyál-te żág-pa* (*nyal te zhag pa*) C., bór-ce (bor ce) W., to lay or put down, opp. to laṅ-te (lang te) etc., to set or place upright, to set on end, e.g. a book. - 2. fig.: *me nyál-wa* (*me nyal wa*) C. to put the fire to bed, i.e. to scrape it together and cover it with ashes; spŭ (spu) snyól-ba (snyol ba) to smooth down the bristling hair, i.e. to abate one's anger; čaṅ (chang), żo (zho), snyól-ba (snyol ba) to allow the beer to ferment, the milk to curdle, in a state of rest (undisturbed).
pf. brnyas (brnyas), 1. to borrow Dzl. མདོ་ (mdo), 12. 14; མདོ་ (mdo), 6. 2. to seize by force, to usurp Sch.

Cs. to borrow; brnyán-po (brnyan po) borrowed; also fig.: borrowed, reflected, gzugs-brnyán (gzugs brnyan) (Lex. snaṅ-brnyán (snang brnyan)) a reflected image प्रतिविम्ब frq.; also image, picture in general; even a little statue Pth.; rmi-lam-gyi gzugs-brnyán (rmi lam gyi gzugs brnyan) vision, visionary image; sgra-brnyán (sgra brnyan), प्रतिश्रुत reflected sound, echo; mgo-brnyán (mgo brnyan) a mask, a fearful apparition Thgy., mgo-brnyán sér-po (mgo brnyan ser po) Schl. 234. - pyag-brnyán (phyag brnyan) servant Cs. - brnyán-poi gos (brnyan poi gos) Cs.: 'a garment marked with the figures (sic) of the rainbow' - brnyán-poi brnyas (brnyan poi brnyas) Lex. interest for a loan, rent for things borrowed Sch.
diligence, painstaking; to take pains Sch.

I. borrowed, v. brnyá-ba (brnya ba). II. 1. to despise, contemn c. dat., frq.; ma brnyás śig (ma brnyas shig) do not despise! Dzl.; brnyas smád-pa (brnyas smad pa) id. Dzl. — contempt, brnyás-pa byèd-pa (brnyas pa byed pa), W. *nya-śè tág-če* (*nya she tag ce*), ccd. to despise, to treat contemptuously, frq.; brnyas-bčos (brnyas bcos) (Thgy. brnyas-čos (brnyas chos)) contempt, scorn.
1. to return, restore, deliver up Cs. 2. sediment.
full Sch.; skyu-gaṅ bsnyug (skyu gang bsnyug) Lex. a full draught (?).

1. to approach, to come near, c. dat., also drùṅ-du (drung du), kó-boi drùṅ-du bsnyen čig (ko boi drung du bsnyen cig) come to me Dzl.; góm-pa re-ré bór-źiṅ ći-ba-la bsnyén-pa ltar (gom pa re re bor zhing chi ba la bsnyen pa ltar) as with every step we come nearer to our death Thgy.; to join, to stick to a person Dzl. — 2. to propitiate, soothe, satisfy, a deity Cs. — 3. to accept, receive, admit W.; bsnyén-par rdzógs-pa (bsnyen par rdzogs pa) to be ordained, consecrated, frq.; c. las (las) by Tar. — dge-bsnyen (dge bsnyen) v. dgé-ba (dge ba). — bsnyen-bkúr (bsnyen bkur) reverence, veneration, respect, byéd-pa (byed pa) to pay one's duty or respect, esp. to the priesthood by various services, ṅá-la bsnyén-pa byás-te (nga la bsnyen pa byas te) Dzl. and elsewh., frq., also bsnyén-źiṅ bkúr-ba (bsnyen zhing bkur ba) Glr., and *nyen kúr-če* (*nyen kur ce*) W.; bsnyén-bkur żu-ba (bsnyen bkur zhu ba) to ask permission for performing such services Mil. — bsnyen-bsgrúb (bsnyen bsgrub) priestly function, religious office, esp. sṅags (sngags) q. v. — bsnyen-gnás (bsnyen gnas) fasting, abstinence; bsnyen-gnás srúṅ-ba (bsnyen gnas srung ba), W. *zúm-če* (*zum ce*), to abstain from food, to fast.
to make grimaces or gesticulations Cs.
